    22â†’// ApplyOpts are options that affect the details of how Terraform will apply a
    23â†’// previously-generated plan.
    24â†’type ApplyOpts struct {
    25â†’	// ExternalProviders is a set of pre-configured provider instances with
    26â†’	// the same purpose as [PlanOpts.ExternalProviders].
    27â†’	//
    28â†’	// Callers must pass providers that are configured in a similar way as
    29â†’	// the providers that were passed when creating the plan that's being
    30â†’	// applied, or the results will be erratic.
    31â†’	ExternalProviders map[addrs.RootProviderConfig]providers.Interface
    32â†’
    33â†’	// SetVariables are the raw values for root module variables as provided
    34â†’	// by the user who is requesting the run, prior to any normalization or
    35â†’	// substitution of defaults. See the documentation for the InputValue
    36â†’	// type for more information on how to correctly populate this.
    37â†’	//
    38â†’	// During the apply phase it's only valid to specify values for input
    39â†’	// values that were declared as ephemeral, because all other input
    40â†’	// values must retain the values that were specified during planning.
    41â†’	SetVariables InputValues
    42â†’}
    43â†’
    44â†’// ApplyOpts creates an [ApplyOpts] with copies of all of the elements that
    45â†’// are expected to propagate from plan to apply when planning and applying
    46â†’// in the same process.
    47â†’//
    48â†’// In practice planning and applying are often separated into two different
    49â†’// executions, in which case callers must retain enough information between
    50â†’// plan and apply to construct an equivalent [ApplyOpts] themselves without
    51â†’// using this function. This is here mainly for convenient internal use such
    52â†’// as in test cases.
    53â†’func (po *PlanOpts) ApplyOpts() *ApplyOpts {
    54â†’	return &ApplyOpts{
    55â†’		ExternalProviders: po.ExternalProviders,
    56â†’	}
    57â†’}
    58â†’
    59â†’// Apply performs the actions described by the given Plan object and returns
    60â†’// the resulting updated state.
    61â†’//
    62â†’// The given configuration *must* be the same configuration that was passed
    63â†’// earlier to Context.Plan in order to create this plan.
    64â†’//
    65â†’// Even if the returned diagnostics contains errors, Apply always returns the
    66â†’// resulting state which is likely to have been partially-updated.
    67â†’//
    68â†’// The [opts] argument may be nil to indicate that no special options are
    69â†’// required. In that case, Apply will use a default set of options. Some
    70â†’// options in [PlanOpts] when creating a plan must be echoed with equivalent
    71â†’// settings during apply, so leaving opts as nil might not be valid for
    72â†’// certain combinations of plan-time options.
    73â†’func (c *Context) Apply(plan *plans.Plan, config *configs.Config, opts *ApplyOpts) (*states.State, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
    74â†’	state, _, diags := c.ApplyAndEval(plan, config, opts)
    75â†’	return state, diags
    76â†’}
    77â†’
    78â†’// ApplyAndEval is like [Context.Apply] except that it additionally makes a
    79â†’// best effort to return a [lang.Scope] which can evaluate expressions in the
    80â†’// root module based on the content of the new state.
    81â†’//
    82â†’// The scope will be nil if the apply process doesn't complete successfully
    83â†’// enough to produce a valid evaluation scope. If the returned state is nil
    84â†’// then the scope will always be nil, but it's also possible for the scope
    85â†’// to be nil even when the state isn't, if the apply didn't complete enough for
    86â†’// the evaluation scope to produce consistent results.
    87â†’func (c *Context) ApplyAndEval(plan *plans.Plan, config *configs.Config, opts *ApplyOpts) (*states.State, *lang.Scope, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
    88â†’	defer c.acquireRun("apply")()
    89â†’	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
    90â†’
    91â†’	if plan == nil {
    92â†’		panic("cannot apply nil plan")
    93â†’	}
    94â†’	log.Printf("[DEBUG] Building and walking apply graph for %s plan", plan.UIMode)
    95â†’
    96â†’	if opts == nil {
    97â†’		opts = &ApplyOpts{}
    98â†’	}
    99â†’
   100â†’	if plan.Errored {
   101â†’		diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   102â†’			tfdiags.Error,
   103â†’			"Cannot apply failed plan",
   104â†’			`The given plan is incomplete due to errors during planning, and so it cannot be applied.`,
   105â†’		))
   106â†’		return nil, nil, diags
   107â†’	}
   108â†’	if !plan.Applyable {
   109â†’		if plan.Changes.Empty() {
   110â†’			// If a plan is not applyable but it didn't have any errors then that
   111â†’			// suggests it was a "no-op" plan, which doesn't really do any
   112â†’			// harm to apply, so we'll just do it but leave ourselves a note
   113â†’			// in the trace log in case it ends up relevant to a bug report.
   114â†’			log.Printf("[TRACE] Applying a no-op plan")
   115â†’		} else {
   116â†’			// This situation isn't something we expect, since our own rules
   117â†’			// for what "applyable" means make this scenario impossible. We'll
   118â†’			// reject it on the assumption that something very strange is
   119â†’			// going on. and so better to halt than do something incorrect.
   120â†’			// This error message is generic and useless because we don't
   121â†’			// expect anyone to ever see it in normal use.
   122â†’			diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   123â†’				tfdiags.Error,
   124â†’				"Cannot apply non-applyable plan",
   125â†’				`The given plan is not applyable. If this seems like a bug in Terraform, then please report it!`,
   126â†’			))
   127â†’			return nil, nil, diags
   128â†’		}
   129â†’	}
   130â†’
   131â†’	// The caller must provide values for all of the "apply-time variables"
   132â†’	// mentioned in the plan, and for no others because the others come from
   133â†’	// the plan itself.
   134â†’	// Apply-time variable check handled by graph walk validation.
   135â†’
   136â†’	for _, rc := range plan.Changes.Resources {
   137â†’		// Import is a no-op change during an apply (all the real action happens during the plan) but we'd
   138â†’		// like to show some helpful output that mirrors the way we show other changes.
   139â†’		if rc.Importing != nil {
   140â†’			hookResourceID := HookResourceIdentity{
   141â†’				Addr:         rc.Addr,
   142â†’				ProviderAddr: rc.ProviderAddr.Provider,
   143â†’			}
   144â†’			for _, h := range c.hooks {
   145â†’				// In future, we may need to call PostApplyImport separately elsewhere in the apply
   146â†’				// operation. For now, though, we'll call Pre and Post hooks together.
   147â†’				h.PreApplyImport(hookResourceID, *rc.Importing)
   148â†’				h.PostApplyImport(hookResourceID, *rc.Importing)
   149â†’			}
   150â†’		}
   151â†’	}
   152â†’
   153â†’	graph, operation, moreDiags := c.applyGraph(plan, config, opts, true)
   154â†’	diags = diags.Append(moreDiags)
   155â†’	if moreDiags.HasErrors() {
   156â†’		return nil, nil, diags
   157â†’	}
   158â†’
   159â†’	moreDiags = checkExternalProviders(config, plan, nil, opts.ExternalProviders)
   160â†’	diags = diags.Append(moreDiags)
   161â†’	if moreDiags.HasErrors() {
   162â†’		return nil, nil, diags
   163â†’	}
   164â†’
   165â†’	schemas, schemaDiags := c.Schemas(config, plan.PriorState)
   166â†’	diags = diags.Append(schemaDiags)
   167â†’	if diags.HasErrors() {
   168â†’		return nil, nil, diags
   169â†’	}
   170â†’
   171â†’	changes, err := plan.Changes.Decode(schemas)
   172â†’	if err != nil {
   173â†’		diags = diags.Append(err)
   174â†’		return nil, nil, diags
   175â†’	}
   176â†’
   177â†’	workingState := plan.PriorState.DeepCopy()
   178â†’	walker, walkDiags := c.walk(graph, operation, &graphWalkOpts{
   179â†’		Config:                  config,
   180â†’		InputState:              workingState,
   181â†’		Changes:                 changes,
   182â†’		Overrides:               plan.Overrides,
   183â†’		ExternalProviderConfigs: opts.ExternalProviders,
   184â†’
   185â†’		DeferralAllowed: true,
   186â†’
   187â†’		// We need to propagate the check results from the plan phase,
   188â†’		// because that will tell us which checkable objects we're expecting
   189â†’		// to see updated results from during the apply step.
   190â†’		PlanTimeCheckResults: plan.Checks,
   191â†’
   192â†’		// We also want to propagate the timestamp from the plan file.
   193â†’		PlanTimeTimestamp: plan.Timestamp,
   194â†’
   195â†’		ProviderFuncResults: providers.NewFunctionResultsTable(plan.ProviderFunctionResults),
   196â†’	})
   197â†’	diags = diags.Append(walker.NonFatalDiagnostics)
   198â†’	diags = diags.Append(walkDiags)
   199â†’
   200â†’	// After the walk is finished, we capture a simplified snapshot of the
   201â†’	// check result data as part of the new state.
   202â†’	walker.State.RecordCheckResults(walker.Checks)
   203â†’
   204â†’	newState := walker.State.Close()
   205â†’	if plan.UIMode == plans.DestroyMode && !diags.HasErrors() {
   206â†’		// NOTE: This is a vestigial violation of the rule that we mustn't
   207â†’		// use plan.UIMode to affect apply-time behavior.
   208â†’		// We ideally ought to just call newState.PruneResourceHusks
   209â†’		// unconditionally here, but we historically didn't and haven't yet
   210â†’		// verified that it'd be safe to do so.
   211â†’		newState.PruneResourceHusks()
   212â†’	}
   213â†’
   214â†’	if len(plan.TargetAddrs) > 0 {
   215â†’		diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   216â†’			tfdiags.Warning,
   217â†’			"Applied changes may be incomplete",
   218â†’			`The plan was created with the -target option in effect, so some changes requested in the configuration may have been ignored and the output values may not be fully updated. Run the following command to verify that no other changes are pending:
   219â†’    terraform plan
   220â†’
   221â†’Note that the -target option is not suitable for routine use, and is provided only for exceptional situations such as recovering from errors or mistakes, or when Terraform specifically suggests to use it as part of an error message.`,
   222â†’		))
   223â†’	}
   224â†’
   225â†’	// FIXME: we cannot check for an empty plan for refresh-only, because root
   226â†’	// outputs are always stored as changes. The final condition of the state
   227â†’	// also depends on some cleanup which happens during the apply walk. It
   228â†’	// would probably make more sense if applying a refresh-only plan were
   229â†’	// simply just returning the planned state and checks, but some extra
   230â†’	// cleanup is going to be needed to make the plan state match what apply
   231â†’	// would do. For now we can copy the checks over which were overwritten
   232â†’	// during the apply walk.
   233â†’	// Despite the intent of UIMode, it must still be used for apply-time
   234â†’	// differences in destroy plans too, so we can make use of that here as
   235â†’	// well.
   236â†’	if plan.UIMode == plans.RefreshOnlyMode {
   237â†’		newState.CheckResults = plan.Checks.DeepCopy()
   238â†’	}
   239â†’
   240â†’	// The caller also gets access to an expression evaluation scope in the
   241â†’	// root module, in case it needs to extract other information using
   242â†’	// expressions, like in "terraform console" or the test harness.
   243â†’	evalScope := evalScopeFromGraphWalk(walker, addrs.RootModuleInstance)
   244â†’
   245â†’	return newState, evalScope, diags
   246â†’}
   247â†’
   248â†’func checkApplyTimeVariables(needed collections.Set[string], gotValues InputValues, config *configs.Config) tfdiags.Diagnostics {
   249â†’	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   250â†’	for name := range needed.All() {
   251â†’		if vv, exists := gotValues[name]; !exists || vv.Value == cty.NilVal || vv.Value.IsNull() {
   252â†’			// This error message assumes that the only possible reason for
   253â†’			// an apply-time variable is because the variable is ephemeral,
   254â†’			// which is true at the time of writing. This error message might
   255â†’			// need to be generalized if we introduce other reasons for
   256â†’			// apply-time variables in future.
   257â†’			diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   258â†’				tfdiags.Error,
   259â†’				"No value for required variable",
   260â†’				fmt.Sprintf("The ephemeral input variable %q was set during the plan phase, and so must also be set during the apply phase.", name),
   261â†’			))
   262â†’		}
   263â†’	}
   264â†’	for name := range gotValues {
   265â†’		if !needed.Has(name) {
   266â†’			// We'll treat this a little differently depending on whether
   267â†’			// the variable is declared as ephemeral or not.
   268â†’			if vc, ok := config.Module.Variables[name]; ok && vc.Ephemeral {
   269â†’				diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   270â†’					tfdiags.Error,
   271â†’					"No value for required variable",
   272â†’					fmt.Sprintf("The ephemeral input variable %q was not set during the plan phase, and so must remain unset during the apply phase.", name),
   273â†’				))
   274â†’			} else {
   275â†’				diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   276â†’					tfdiags.Error,
   277â†’					"Unexpected new value for variable",
   278â†’					fmt.Sprintf("Input variable %q is non-ephemeral, so its value was fixed during planning and cannot be reset during apply.", name),
   279â†’				))
   280â†’			}
   281â†’		}
   282â†’	}
   283â†’	return diags
   284â†’}

   286â†’func (c *Context) applyGraph(plan *plans.Plan, config *configs.Config, opts *ApplyOpts, validate bool) (*Graph, walkOperation, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   287â†’	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   288â†’
   289â†’	variables := InputValues{}
   290â†’	for name, dyVal := range plan.VariableValues {
   291â†’		val, err := dyVal.Decode(cty.DynamicPseudoType)
   292â†’		if err != nil {
   293â†’			diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
   294â†’				tfdiags.Error,
   295â†’				"Invalid variable value in plan",
   296â†’				fmt.Sprintf("Invalid value for variable %q recorded in plan file: %s.", name, err),
   297â†’			))
   298â†’			continue
   299â†’		}
   300â†’		if pvm, ok := plan.VariableMarks[name]; ok {
   301â†’			val = val.MarkWithPaths(pvm)
   302â†’		}
   303â†’
   304â†’		variables[name] = &InputValue{
   305â†’			Value:      val,
   306â†’			SourceType: ValueFromPlan,
   307â†’		}
   308â†’	}
   309â†’	// Apply-time variables need to be merged in too.
   310â†’	// FIXME: We should check that all of these match declared variables and
   311â†’	// that all of them are declared as ephemeral, because all non-ephemeral
   312â†’	// variables are supposed to come exclusively from plan.VariableValues.
   313â†’	if opts != nil {
   314â†’		for n, vv := range opts.SetVariables {
   315â†’			variables[n] = vv
   316â†’		}
   317â†’	}
   318â†’	if diags.HasErrors() {
   319â†’		return nil, walkApply, diags
   320â†’	}
   321â†’
   322â†’	// The plan.VariableValues field only records variables that were actually
   323â†’	// set by the caller in the PlanOpts, so we may need to provide
   324â†’	// placeholders for any other variables that the user didn't set, in
   325â†’	// which case Terraform will once again use the default value from the
   326â†’	// configuration when we visit these variables during the graph walk.
   327â†’	for name := range config.Module.Variables {
   328â†’		if _, ok := variables[name]; ok {
   329â†’			continue
   330â†’		}
   331â†’		variables[name] = &InputValue{
   332â†’			Value:      cty.NilVal,
   333â†’			SourceType: ValueFromPlan,
   334â†’		}
   335â†’	}
   336â†’
   337â†’	operation := walkApply
   338â†’	if plan.UIMode == plans.DestroyMode {
   339â†’		// FIXME: Due to differences in how objects must be handled in the
   340â†’		// graph and evaluated during a complete destroy, we must continue to
   341â†’		// use plans.DestroyMode to switch on this behavior. If all objects
   342â†’		// which require special destroy handling can be tracked in the plan,
   343â†’		// then this switch will no longer be needed and we can remove the
   344â†’		// walkDestroy operation mode.
   345â†’		// TODO: Audit that and remove walkDestroy as an operation mode.
   346â†’		operation = walkDestroy
   347â†’	}
   348â†’
   349â†’	var externalProviderConfigs map[addrs.RootProviderConfig]providers.Interface
   350â†’	if opts != nil {
   351â†’		externalProviderConfigs = opts.ExternalProviders
   352â†’	}
   353â†’
   354â†’	graph, moreDiags := (&ApplyGraphBuilder{
   355â†’		Config:                  config,
   356â†’		Changes:                 plan.Changes,
   357â†’		DeferredChanges:         plan.DeferredResources,
   358â†’		State:                   plan.PriorState,
   359â†’		RootVariableValues:      variables,
   360â†’		ExternalProviderConfigs: externalProviderConfigs,
   361â†’		Plugins:                 c.plugins,
   362â†’		Targets:                 plan.TargetAddrs,
   363â†’		ForceReplace:            plan.ForceReplaceAddrs,
   364â†’		Operation:               operation,
   365â†’		ExternalReferences:      plan.ExternalReferences,
   366â†’		Overrides:               plan.Overrides,
   367â†’	}).Build(addrs.RootModuleInstance)
   368â†’	diags = diags.Append(moreDiags)
   369â†’	if moreDiags.HasErrors() {
   370â†’		return nil, walkApply, diags
   371â†’	}
   372â†’
   373â†’	return graph, operation, diags
   374â†’}
   375â†’
   376â†’// ApplyGraphForUI is a last vestage of graphs in the public interface of
   377â†’// Context (as opposed to graphs as an implementation detail) intended only for
   378â†’// use by the "terraform graph" command when asked to render an apply-time
   379â†’// graph.
   380â†’//
   381â†’// The result of this is intended only for rendering ot the user as a dot
   382â†’// graph, and so may change in future in order to make the result more useful
   383â†’// in that context, even if drifts away from the physical graph that Terraform
   384â†’// Core currently uses as an implementation detail of applying.
   385â†’func (c *Context) ApplyGraphForUI(plan *plans.Plan, config *configs.Config) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {

    15â†’// HookAction is an enum of actions that can be taken as a result of a hook
    16â†’// callback. This allows you to modify the behavior of Terraform at runtime.
    17â†’type HookAction byte
    18â†’
    19â†’const (
    20â†’	// HookActionContinue continues with processing as usual.
    21â†’	HookActionContinue HookAction = iota
    22â†’
    23â†’	// HookActionHalt halts immediately: no more hooks are processed
    24â†’	// and the action that Terraform was about to take is cancelled.
    25â†’	HookActionHalt
    26â†’)
    27â†’
    28â†’// HookResourceIdentity is passed to Hook interface methods to fully identify
    29â†’// the resource instance being operated on. It currently includes the resource
    30â†’// address and the provider address.
    31â†’type HookResourceIdentity struct {
    32â†’	Addr         addrs.AbsResourceInstance
    33â†’	ProviderAddr addrs.Provider
    34â†’}
    35â†’
    36â†’// Hook is the interface that must be implemented to hook into various
    37â†’// parts of Terraform, allowing you to inspect or change behavior at runtime.
    38â†’//
    39â†’// There are MANY hook points into Terraform. If you only want to implement
    40â†’// some hook points, but not all (which is the likely case), then embed the
    41â†’// NilHook into your struct, which implements all of the interface but does
    42â†’// nothing. Then, override only the functions you want to implement.
    43â†’type Hook interface {
    44â†’	// PreApply and PostApply are called before and after an action for a
    45â†’	// single instance is applied. The error argument in PostApply is the
    46â†’	// error, if any, that was returned from the provider Apply call itself.
    47â†’	PreApply(id HookResourceIdentity, dk addrs.DeposedKey, action plans.Action, priorState, plannedNewState cty.Value) (HookAction, error)
    48â†’	PostApply(id HookResourceIdentity, dk addrs.DeposedKey, newState cty.Value, err error) (HookAction, error)
    49â†’
    50â†’	// PreDiff and PostDiff are called before and after a provider is given
    51â†’	// the opportunity to customize the proposed new state to produce the
    52â†’	// planned new state.
    53â†’	PreDiff(id HookResourceIdentity, dk addrs.DeposedKey, priorState, proposedNewState cty.Value) (HookAction, error)
    54â†’	PostDiff(id HookResourceIdentity, dk addrs.DeposedKey, action plans.Action, priorState, plannedNewState cty.Value) (HookAction, error)
    55â†’
    56â†’	// The provisioning hooks signal both the overall start end end of
    57â†’	// provisioning for a particular instance and of each of the individual
    58â†’	// configured provisioners for each instance. The sequence of these
    59â†’	// for a given instance might look something like this:
    60â†’	//
    61â†’	//          PreProvisionInstance(aws_instance.foo[1], ...)
    62â†’	//      PreProvisionInstanceStep(aws_instance.foo[1], "file")
    63â†’	//     PostProvisionInstanceStep(aws_instance.foo[1], "file", nil)
    64â†’	//      PreProvisionInstanceStep(aws_instance.foo[1], "remote-exec")
    65â†’	//               ProvisionOutput(aws_instance.foo[1], "remote-exec", "Installing foo...")
    66â†’	//               ProvisionOutput(aws_instance.foo[1], "remote-exec", "Configuring bar...")
    67â†’	//     PostProvisionInstanceStep(aws_instance.foo[1], "remote-exec", nil)
    68â†’	//         PostProvisionInstance(aws_instance.foo[1], ...)
    69â†’	//
    70â†’	// ProvisionOutput is called with output sent back by the provisioners.
    71â†’	// This will be called multiple times as output comes in, with each call
    72â†’	// representing one line of output. It cannot control whether the
    73â†’	// provisioner continues running.
    74â†’	PreProvisionInstance(id HookResourceIdentity, state cty.Value) (HookAction, error)
    75â†’	PostProvisionInstance(id HookResourceIdentity, state cty.Value) (HookAction, error)
    76â†’	PreProvisionInstanceStep(id HookResourceIdentity, typeName string) (HookAction, error)
    77â†’	PostProvisionInstanceStep(id HookResourceIdentity, typeName string, err error) (HookAction, error)
    78â†’	ProvisionOutput(id HookResourceIdentity, typeName string, line string)
    79â†’
    80â†’	// PreRefresh and PostRefresh are called before and after a single
    81â†’	// resource state is refreshed, respectively.
    82â†’	PreRefresh(id HookResourceIdentity, dk addrs.DeposedKey, priorState cty.Value) (HookAction, error)
    83â†’	PostRefresh(id HookResourceIdentity, dk addrs.DeposedKey, priorState cty.Value, newState cty.Value) (HookAction, error)
    84â†’
    85â†’	// PreImportState and PostImportState are called before and after
    86â†’	// (respectively) each state import operation for a given resource address when
    87â†’	// using the legacy import command.
    88â†’	PreImportState(id HookResourceIdentity, importID string) (HookAction, error)
    89â†’	PostImportState(id HookResourceIdentity, imported []providers.ImportedResource) (HookAction, error)
    90â†’
    91â†’	// PrePlanImport and PostPlanImport are called during a plan before and after planning to import
    92â†’	// a new resource using the configuration-driven import workflow.
    93â†’	PrePlanImport(id HookResourceIdentity, importID string) (HookAction, error)
    94â†’	PostPlanImport(id HookResourceIdentity, imported []providers.ImportedResource) (HookAction, error)
    95â†’
    96â†’	// PreApplyImport and PostApplyImport are called during an apply for each imported resource when
    97â†’	// using the configuration-driven import workflow.
    98â†’	PreApplyImport(id HookResourceIdentity, importing plans.ImportingSrc) (HookAction, error)
    99â†’	PostApplyImport(id HookResourceIdentity, importing plans.ImportingSrc) (HookAction, error)
   100â†’
   101â†’	// PreEphemeralOp and PostEphemeralOp are called during an operation on ephemeral resource
   102â†’	// such as opening, renewal or closing
   103â†’	PreEphemeralOp(id HookResourceIdentity, action plans.Action) (HookAction, error)
   104â†’	PostEphemeralOp(id HookResourceIdentity, action plans.Action, opErr error) (HookAction, error)
   105â†’
   106â†’	// Stopping is called if an external signal requests that Terraform
   107â†’	// gracefully abort an operation in progress.
   108â†’	//
   109â†’	// This notification might suggest that the user wants Terraform to exit
   110â†’	// ASAP and in that case it's possible that if Terraform runs for too much
   111â†’	// longer then it'll get killed un-gracefully, and so this hook could be
   112â†’	// an opportunity to persist any transient data that would be lost under
   113â†’	// a subsequent kill signal. However, implementations must take care to do
   114â†’	// so in a way that won't cause corruption if the process _is_ killed while
   115â†’	// this hook is still running.
   116â†’	//
   117â†’	// This hook cannot control whether Terraform continues, because the
   118â†’	// graceful shutdown process is typically already running by the time this
   119â†’	// function is called.
   120â†’	Stopping()
   121â†’
   122â†’	// PostStateUpdate is called each time the state is updated. The caller must
   123â†’	// coordinate a lock for the state if necessary, such that the Hook may
   124â†’	// access it freely without any need for additional locks to protect from
   125â†’	// concurrent writes. Implementations which modify or retain the state after
   126â†’	// the call has returned must copy the state.
   127â†’	PostStateUpdate(new *states.State) (HookAction, error)
   128â†’}

   131â†’// simplify implementing hooks. You can embed this into your Hook implementation

    50â†’	// PreDiff and PostDiff are called before and after a provider is given
    51â†’	// the opportunity to customize the proposed new state to produce the
    52â†’	// planned new state.

    54â†’	PostDiff(id HookResourceIdentity, dk addrs.DeposedKey, action plans.Action, priorState, plannedNewState cty.Value) (HookAction, error)
    55â†’

    57â†’	// provisioning for a particular instance and of each of the individual
    58â†’	// configured provisioners for each instance. The sequence of these
    59â†’	// for a given instance might look something like this:
    60â†’	//
    61â†’	//          PreProvisionInstance(aws_instance.foo[1], ...)
    62â†’	//      PreProvisionInstanceStep(aws_instance.foo[1], "file")
    63â†’	//     PostProvisionInstanceStep(aws_instance.foo[1], "file", nil)
    64â†’	//      PreProvisionInstanceStep(aws_instance.foo[1], "remote-exec")
    65â†’	//               ProvisionOutput(aws_instance.foo[1], "remote-exec", "Installing foo...")
    66â†’	//               ProvisionOutput(aws_instance.foo[1], "remote-exec", "Configuring bar...")
    67â†’	//     PostProvisionInstanceStep(aws_instance.foo[1], "remote-exec", nil)
    68â†’	//         PostProvisionInstance(aws_instance.foo[1], ...)
    69â†’	//
    70â†’	// ProvisionOutput is called with output sent back by the provisioners.

    72â†’	// representing one line of output. It cannot control whether the

    77â†’	PostProvisionInstanceStep(id HookResourceIdentity, typeName string, err error) (HookAction, error)

    97â†’	// using the configuration-driven import workflow.

    99â†’	PostApplyImport(id HookResourceIdentity, importing plans.ImportingSrc) (HookAction, error)
   100â†’
   101â†’	// PreEphemeralOp and PostEphemeralOp are called during an operation on ephemeral resource
   102â†’	// such as opening, renewal or closing
   103â†’	PreEphemeralOp(id HookResourceIdentity, action plans.Action) (HookAction, error)
   104â†’	PostEphemeralOp(id HookResourceIdentity, action plans.Action, opErr error) (HookAction, error)

   106â†’	// Stopping is called if an external signal requests that Terraform
   107â†’	// gracefully abort an operation in progress.

   112â†’	// an opportunity to persist any transient data that would be lost under

   123â†’	// coordinate a lock for the state if necessary, such that the Hook may

    28â†’
    29â†’// Evaluator provides the necessary contextual data for evaluating expressions
    30â†’// for a particular walk operation.
    31â†’type Evaluator struct {
    32â†’	// Operation defines what type of operation this evaluator is being used
    33â†’	// for.
    34â†’	Operation walkOperation
    35â†’
    36â†’	// Meta is contextual metadata about the current operation.
    37â†’	Meta *ContextMeta
    38â†’
    39â†’	// Config is the root node in the configuration tree.
    40â†’	Config *configs.Config
    41â†’
    42â†’	// Instances tracks the dynamic instances that are associated with each
    43â†’	// module call or resource. The graph walk gradually registers the
    44â†’	// set of instances for each object within the graph nodes for those
    45â†’	// objects, and so as long as the graph has been built correctly the
    46â†’	// set of instances for an object should always be available by the time
    47â†’	// we're evaluating expressions that refer to it.
    48â†’	Instances *instances.Expander
    49â†’
    50â†’	// NamedValues is where we keep the values of already-evaluated input
    51â†’	// variables, local values, and output values.
    52â†’	NamedValues *namedvals.State
    53â†’
    54â†’	// EphemeralResources tracks the currently-open instances of any ephemeral
    55â†’	// resources.
    56â†’	EphemeralResources *ephemeral.Resources
    57â†’
    58â†’	// Deferrals tracks resources and modules that have had either their
    59â†’	// expansion or their specific planned actions deferred to a future
    60â†’	// plan/apply round.
    61â†’	Deferrals *deferring.Deferred
    62â†’
    63â†’	// Plugins is the library of available plugin components (providers and
    64â†’	// provisioners) that we have available to help us evaluate expressions
    65â†’	// that interact with plugin-provided objects.
    66â†’	//
    67â†’	// From this we only access the schemas of the plugins, and don't otherwise
    68â†’	// interact with plugin instances.
    69â†’	Plugins *contextPlugins
    70â†’
    71â†’	// State is the current state, embedded in a wrapper that ensures that
    72â†’	// it can be safely accessed and modified concurrently.
    73â†’	State *states.SyncState
    74â†’
    75â†’	// Changes is the set of proposed changes, embedded in a wrapper that
    76â†’	// ensures they can be safely accessed and modified concurrently.
    77â†’	Changes *plans.ChangesSync
    78â†’
    79â†’	PlanTimestamp time.Time
    80â†’}
    81â†’
    82â†’// Scope creates an evaluation scope for the given module path and optional
    83â†’// resource.
    84â†’//
    85â†’// If the "self" argument is nil then the "self" object is not available
    86â†’// in evaluated expressions. Otherwise, it behaves as an alias for the given
    87â†’// address.
    88â†’func (e *Evaluator) Scope(data lang.Data, self addrs.Referenceable, source addrs.Referenceable, extFuncs lang.ExternalFuncs) *lang.Scope {
    89â†’	return &lang.Scope{
    90â†’		Data:          data,
    91â†’		ParseRef:      addrs.ParseRef,
    92â†’		SelfAddr:      self,
    93â†’		SourceAddr:    source,
    94â†’		PureOnly:      e.Operation != walkApply && e.Operation != walkDestroy && e.Operation != walkEval,
    95â†’		BaseDir:       ".", // Always current working directory for now.
    96â†’		PlanTimestamp: e.PlanTimestamp,
    97â†’		ExternalFuncs: extFuncs,
    98â†’	}
    99â†’}
   100â†’
   101â†’// evaluationStateData is an implementation of lang.Data that resolves
   102â†’// references primarily (but not exclusively) using information from a State.
   103â†’type evaluationStateData struct {
   104â†’	*evaluationData
   105â†’
   106â†’	// ModulePath is the path through the dynamic module tree to the module
   107â†’	// that references will be resolved relative to.
   108â†’	ModulePath addrs.ModuleInstance
   109â†’
   110â†’	// InstanceKeyData describes the values, if any, that are accessible due
   111â†’	// to repetition of a containing object using "count" or "for_each"
   112â†’	// arguments. (It is _not_ used for the for_each inside "dynamic" blocks,
   113â†’	// since the user specifies in that case which variable name to locally
   114â†’	// shadow.)
   115â†’	InstanceKeyData InstanceKeyEvalData
   116â†’
   117â†’	// Operation records the type of walk the evaluationStateData is being used
   118â†’	// for.
   119â†’	Operation walkOperation
   120â†’}
   121â†’
   122â†’// InstanceKeyEvalData is the old name for instances.RepetitionData, aliased
   123â†’// here for compatibility. In new code, use instances.RepetitionData instead.
   124â†’type InstanceKeyEvalData = instances.RepetitionData
   125â†’
   126â†’// EvalDataForInstanceKey constructs a suitable InstanceKeyEvalData for
   127â†’// evaluating in a context that has the given instance key.
   128â†’//
   129â†’// The forEachMap argument can be nil when preparing for evaluation
   130â†’// in a context where each.value is prohibited, such as a destroy-time
   131â†’// provisioner. In that case, the returned EachValue will always be
   132â†’// cty.NilVal.
   133â†’func EvalDataForInstanceKey(key addrs.InstanceKey, forEachMap map[string]cty.Value) InstanceKeyEvalData {
   134â†’	var evalData InstanceKeyEvalData
   135â†’	if key == nil {
   136â†’		return evalData
   137â†’	}
   138â†’
   139â†’	keyValue := key.Value()
   140â†’	switch keyValue.Type() {
   141â†’	case cty.String:
   142â†’		evalData.EachKey = keyValue
   143â†’		evalData.EachValue = forEachMap[keyValue.AsString()]
   144â†’	case cty.Number:
   145â†’		evalData.CountIndex = keyValue
   146â†’	}
   147â†’	return evalData
   148â†’}
   149â†’
   150â†’// EvalDataForNoInstanceKey is a value of InstanceKeyData that sets no instance
   151â†’// key values at all, suitable for use in contexts where no keyed instance
   152â†’// is relevant.
   153â†’var EvalDataForNoInstanceKey = InstanceKeyEvalData{}
   154â†’
   155â†’// evaluationStateData must implement lang.Data
   156â†’var _ lang.Data = (*evaluationStateData)(nil)
   157â†’
   158â†’// StaticValidateReferences calls [Evaluator.StaticValidateReferences] on
   159â†’// the evaluator embedded in this data object, using this data object's
   160â†’// static module path.
   161â†’func (d *evaluationStateData) StaticValidateReferences(refs []*addrs.Reference, self addrs.Referenceable, source addrs.Referenceable) tfdiags.Diagnostics {
   162â†’	return d.Evaluator.StaticValidateReferences(refs, d.ModulePath.Module(), self, source)
   163â†’}
   164â†’
   165â†’func (d *evaluationStateData) GetCountAttr(addr addrs.CountAttr, rng tfdiags.SourceRange) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   166â†’	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   167â†’	switch addr.Name {
   168â†’
   169â†’	case "index":
   170â†’		idxVal := d.InstanceKeyData.CountIndex
   171â†’		if idxVal == cty.NilVal {
   172â†’			diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   173â†’				Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   174â†’				Summary:  `Reference to "count" in non-counted context`,
   175â†’				Detail:   `The "count" object can only be used in "module", "resource", and "data" blocks, and only when the "count" argument is set.`,
   176â†’				Subject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),
   177â†’			})
   178â†’			return cty.UnknownVal(cty.Number), diags
   179â†’		}
   180â†’		return idxVal, diags
   181â†’
   182â†’	default:
   183â†’		diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   184â†’			Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   185â†’			Summary:  `Invalid "count" attribute`,
   186â†’			Detail:   fmt.Sprintf(`The "count" object does not have an attribute named %q. The only supported attribute is count.index, which is the index of each instance of a resource block that has the "count" argument set.`, addr.Name),
   187â†’			Subject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),
   188â†’		})
   189â†’		return cty.DynamicVal, diags
   190â†’	}
   191â†’}
   192â†’
   193â†’func (d *evaluationStateData) GetForEachAttr(addr addrs.ForEachAttr, rng tfdiags.SourceRange) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   194â†’	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   195â†’	var returnVal cty.Value
   196â†’	switch addr.Name {
   197â†’
   198â†’	case "key":
   199â†’		returnVal = d.InstanceKeyData.EachKey
   200â†’	case "value":
   201â†’		returnVal = d.InstanceKeyData.EachValue
   202â†’
   203â†’		if returnVal == cty.NilVal {
   204â†’			diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   205â†’				Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   206â†’				Summary:  `each.value cannot be used in this context`,
   207â†’				Detail:   `A reference to "each.value" has been used in a context in which it is unavailable, such as when the configuration no longer contains the value in its "for_each" expression. Remove this reference to each.value in your configuration to work around this error.`,
   208â†’				Subject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),
   209â†’			})
   210â†’			return cty.UnknownVal(cty.DynamicPseudoType), diags
   211â†’		}
   212â†’	default:
   213â†’		diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   214â†’			Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   215â†’			Summary:  `Invalid "each" attribute`,
   216â†’			Detail:   fmt.Sprintf(`The "each" object does not have an attribute named %q. The supported attributes are each.key and each.value, the current key and value pair of the "for_each" attribute set.`, addr.Name),
   217â†’			Subject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),
   218â†’		})
   219â†’		return cty.DynamicVal, diags
   220â†’	}
   221â†’
   222â†’	if returnVal == cty.NilVal {
   223â†’		diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   224â†’			Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   225â†’			Summary:  `Reference to "each" in context without for_each`,
   226â†’			Detail:   `The "each" object can be used only in "module" or "resource" blocks, and only when the "for_each" argument is set.`,
   227â†’			Subject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),
   228â†’		})
   229â†’		return cty.UnknownVal(cty.DynamicPseudoType), diags
   230â†’	}
   231â†’	return returnVal, diags
   232â†’}

   234â†’func (d *evaluationStateData) GetInputVariable(addr addrs.InputVariable, rng tfdiags.SourceRange) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   235â†’	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   236â†’
   237â†’	// First we'll make sure the requested value is declared in configuration,
   238â†’	// so we can produce a nice message if not.
   239â†’	moduleConfig := d.Evaluator.Config.DescendantForInstance(d.ModulePath)
   240â†’	if moduleConfig == nil {
   241â†’		// should never happen, since we can't be evaluating in a module
   242â†’		// that wasn't mentioned in configuration.
   243â†’		panic(fmt.Sprintf("input variable read from %s, which has no configuration", d.ModulePath))
   244â†’	}
   245â†’
   246â†’	config := moduleConfig.Module.Variables[addr.Name]
   247â†’	if config != nil {
   248â†’		var suggestions []string
   249â†’		for k := range moduleConfig.Module.Variables {
   250â†’			suggestions = append(suggestions, k)
   251â†’		}
   252â†’		suggestion := didyoumean.NameSuggestion(addr.Name, suggestions)
   253â†’		if suggestion != "" {
   254â†’			suggestion = fmt.Sprintf(" Did you mean %q?", suggestion)
   255â†’		} else {
   256â†’			suggestion = fmt.Sprintf(" This variable can be declared with a variable %q {} block.", addr.Name)
   257â†’		}
   258â†’
   259â†’		diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   260â†’			Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   261â†’			Summary:  `Reference to undeclared input variable`,
   262â†’			Detail:   fmt.Sprintf(`An input variable with the name %q has not been declared.%s`, addr.Name, suggestion),
   263â†’			Subject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),
   264â†’		})
   265â†’		return cty.DynamicVal, diags
   266â†’	}
   267â†’
   268â†’	// During the validate walk, input variables are always unknown so
   269â†’	// that we are validating the configuration for all possible input values
   270â†’	// rather than for a specific set. Checking against a specific set of
   271â†’	// input values then happens during the plan walk.
   272â†’	//
   273â†’	// This is important because otherwise the validation walk will tend to be
   274â†’	// overly strict, requiring expressions throughout the configuration to
   275â†’	// be complicated to accommodate all possible inputs, whereas returning
   276â†’	// unknown here allows for simpler patterns like using input values as
   277â†’	// guards to broadly enable/disable resources, avoid processing things
   278â†’	// that are disabled, etc. Terraform's static validation leans towards
   279â†’	// being liberal in what it accepts because the subsequent plan walk has
   280â†’	// more information available and so can be more conservative.
   281â†’	if d.Operation != walkValidate {
   282â†’		// We should still capture the statically-configured marks during
   283â†’		// the validate walk.
   284â†’		ret := cty.UnknownVal(config.Type)
   285â†’		if config.Sensitive {
   286â†’			ret = ret.Mark(marks.Sensitive)
   287â†’		}
   288â†’		if config.Ephemeral {
   289â†’			ret = ret.Mark(marks.Ephemeral)
   290â†’		}
   291â†’		return ret, diags
   292â†’	}
   293â†’
   294â†’	val := d.Evaluator.NamedValues.GetInputVariableValue(d.ModulePath.InputVariable(addr.Name))
   295â†’
   296â†’	// Mark if sensitive and/or ephemeral
   297â†’	if config.Sensitive {
   298â†’		val = val.Mark(marks.Sensitive)
   299â†’	}
   300â†’	if config.Ephemeral {
   301â†’		val = val.Mark(marks.Ephemeral)
   302â†’	}
   303â†’
   304â†’	return val, diags
   305â†’}

   307â†’func (d *evaluationStateData) GetLocalValue(addr addrs.LocalValue, rng tfdiags.SourceRange) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   308â†’	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   309â†’
   310â†’	// First we'll make sure the requested value is declared in configuration,
   311â†’	// so we can produce a nice message if not.
   312â†’	moduleConfig := d.Evaluator.Config.DescendantForInstance(d.ModulePath)
   313â†’	if moduleConfig == nil {
   314â†’		// should never happen, since we can't be evaluating in a module
   315â†’		// that wasn't mentioned in configuration.
   316â†’		panic(fmt.Sprintf("local value read from %s, which has no configuration", d.ModulePath))
   317â†’	}
   318â†’
   319â†’	config := moduleConfig.Module.Locals[addr.Name]
   320â†’	if config == nil {
   321â†’		var suggestions []string
   322â†’		for k := range moduleConfig.Module.Locals {
   323â†’			suggestions = append(suggestions, k)
   324â†’		}
   325â†’		suggestion := didyoumean.NameSuggestion(addr.Name, suggestions)
   326â†’		if suggestion != "" {
   327â†’			suggestion = fmt.Sprintf(" Did you mean %q?", suggestion)
   328â†’		}
   329â†’
   330â†’		diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   331â†’			Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   332â†’			Summary:  `Reference to undeclared local value`,
   333â†’			Detail:   fmt.Sprintf(`A local value with the name %q has not been declared.%s`, addr.Name, suggestion),
   334â†’			Subject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),
   335â†’		})
   336â†’		return cty.DynamicVal, diags
   337â†’	}
   338â†’
   339â†’	val := d.Evaluator.NamedValues.GetLocalValue(addr.Absolute(d.ModulePath))
   340â†’	return val, diags
   341â†’}

   343â†’func (d *evaluationStateData) GetModule(addr addrs.ModuleCall, rng tfdiags.SourceRange) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   344â†’	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   345â†’
   346â†’	// Output results live in the module that declares them, which is one of
   347â†’	// the child module instances of our current module path.
   348â†’	moduleAddr := d.ModulePath.Module().Child(addr.Name)
   349â†’	absAddr := addr.Absolute(d.ModulePath)
   350â†’
   351â†’	parentCfg := d.Evaluator.Config.DescendantForInstance(d.ModulePath)
   352â†’	callConfig, ok := parentCfg.Module.ModuleCalls[addr.Name]
   353â†’	if !ok {
   354â†’		diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   355â†’			Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   356â†’			Summary:  `Reference to undeclared module`,
   357â†’			Detail:   fmt.Sprintf(`The configuration contains no %s.`, moduleAddr),
   358â†’			Subject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),
   359â†’		})
   360â†’		return cty.DynamicVal, diags
   361â†’	}
   362â†’
   363â†’	// We'll consult the configuration to see what output names we are
   364â†’	// expecting, so we can ensure the resulting object is of the expected
   365â†’	// type even if our data is incomplete for some reason.
   366â†’	moduleConfig := d.Evaluator.Config.Descendant(moduleAddr)
   367â†’	if moduleConfig == nil {
   368â†’		// should never happen, since we have a valid module call above, this
   369â†’		// should be caught during static validation.
   370â†’		panic(fmt.Sprintf("output value read from %s, which has no configuration", moduleAddr))
   371â†’	}
   372â†’	outputConfigs := moduleConfig.Module.Outputs
   373â†’
   374â†’	// We don't do instance expansion during validation, and so we need to
   375â†’	// return an unknown value. Technically we should always return
   376â†’	// cty.DynamicVal here because the final value during plan will always
   377â†’	// be an object or tuple type with unpredictable attributes/elements,
   378â†’	// but because we never actually carry values forward from validation to
   379â†’	// planning we lie a little here and return unknown list and map types,
   380â†’	// just to give us more opportunities to catch author mistakes during
   381â†’	// validation.
   382â†’	//
   383â†’	// This means that in practice any expression that refers to a module
   384â†’	// call must be written to be valid for either a collection type or
   385â†’	// structural type of similar kind, so that it can be considered as
   386â†’	// valid during both the validate and plan walks.
   387â†’	if d.Operation == walkValidate {
   388â†’		atys := make(map[string]cty.Type, len(outputConfigs))
   389â†’		for name := range outputConfigs {
   390â†’			atys[name] = cty.DynamicPseudoType // output values are dynamically-typed
   391â†’		}
   392â†’		instTy := cty.Object(atys)
   393â†’
   394â†’		switch {
   395â†’		case callConfig.Count != nil:
   396â†’			return cty.UnknownVal(cty.List(instTy)), diags
   397â†’		case callConfig.ForEach != nil:
   398â†’			return cty.UnknownVal(cty.Map(instTy)), diags
   399â†’		default:
   400â†’			return cty.UnknownVal(instTy), diags
   401â†’		}
   402â†’	}
   403â†’
   404â†’	// For all other walk types, we proceed to dynamic evaluation of individual
   405â†’	// instances, using the global instance expander. An earlier graph node
   406â†’	// should always have registered the expansion of this module call before
   407â†’	// we get here, unless there's a bug in the graph builders.
   408â†’	allInstances := d.Evaluator.Instances
   409â†’	instKeyType, instKeys, known := allInstances.ExpandAbsModuleCall(absAddr)
   410â†’	if !known {
   411â†’		// If we don't know which instances exist then we can't really predict
   412â†’		// anything at all. We can't even predict the return type based on
   413â†’		// instKeyType because output values are dynamically-typed and so
   414â†’		// our final result will always be an object or tuple type whose
   415â†’		// attribute/element count we cannot predict.
   416â†’		return cty.DynamicVal, diags
   417â†’	}
   418â†’
   419â†’	instanceObjVal := func(instKey addrs.InstanceKey) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   420â†’		// This function must always return a valid value, even if it's
   421â†’		// just a cty.DynamicVal placeholder accompanying error diagnostics.
   422â†’		var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   423â†’
   424â†’		namedVals := d.Evaluator.NamedValues
   425â†’		moduleInstAddr := absAddr.Instance(instKey)
   426â†’		attrs := make(map[string]cty.Value, len(outputConfigs))
   427â†’		for name := range outputConfigs {
   428â†’			outputAddr := moduleInstAddr.OutputValue(name)
   429â†’
   430â†’			// Although we do typically expect the graph dependencies to
   431â†’			// ensure that values get registered before they are needed,
   432â†’			// we track depedencies with specific output values where
   433â†’			// possible, instead of with entire module calls, and so
   434â†’			// in this specific case it's valid for some of this call's
   435â†’			// output values to not be known yet, with the graph builder
   436â†’			// being responsible for making sure that no expression
   437â†’			// in the configuration can actually observe that.
   438â†’			if !namedVals.HasOutputValue(outputAddr) {
   439â†’				attrs[name] = cty.DynamicVal
   440â†’				continue
   441â†’			}
   442â†’			outputVal := namedVals.GetOutputValue(outputAddr)
   443â†’			attrs[name] = outputVal
   444â†’		}
   445â†’
   446â†’		return cty.ObjectVal(attrs), diags
   447â†’	}
   448â†’
   449â†’	switch instKeyType {
   450â†’
   451â†’	case addrs.NoKeyType:
   452â†’		// In this case we should always have exactly one instance that
   453â†’		// is addrs.NoKey. If not then there's a bug in the [instances.Expander]
   454â†’		// implementation.
   455â†’		if len(instKeys) != 1 {
   456â†’			panic(fmt.Sprintf("module call has no instance key type but has %d instances (should be 1)", len(instKeys)))
   457â†’		}
   458â†’		ret, moreDiags := instanceObjVal(instKeys[0])
   459â†’		diags = diags.Append(moreDiags)
   460â†’		return ret, diags
   461â†’
   462â†’	case addrs.IntKeyType:
   463â†’		// We can assume that the instance keys are in ascending numerical order
   464â†’		// and are consecutive, per the contract of allInstances.ExpandModuleCall.
   465â†’		elems := make([]cty.Value, 0, len(instKeys))
   466â†’		for _, instKey := range instKeys {
   467â†’			instVal, moreDiags := instanceObjVal(instKey)
   468â†’			elems = append(elems, instVal)
   469â†’			diags = diags.Append(moreDiags)
   470â†’		}
   471â†’		return cty.TupleVal(elems), diags
   472â†’
   473â†’	case addrs.StringKeyType:
   474â†’		attrs := make(map[string]cty.Value, len(instKeys))
   475â†’		for _, instKey := range instKeys {
   476â†’			instVal, moreDiags := instanceObjVal(instKey)
   477â†’			attrs[string(instKey.(addrs.StringKey))] = instVal
   478â†’			diags = diags.Append(moreDiags)
   479â†’		}
   480â†’		return cty.ObjectVal(attrs), diags
   481â†’
   482â†’	default:
   483â†’		diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   484â†’			Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   485â†’			Summary:  `Unsupported instance key type`,
   486â†’			Detail: fmt.Sprintf(
   487â†’				`Module call %s has instance key type %#v, which is not supported by the expression evaluator. This is a bug in Terraform.`,
   488â†’				absAddr, instKeyType,
   489â†’			),
   490â†’			Subject: rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),
   491â†’		})
   492â†’		return cty.DynamicVal, diags
   493â†’	}
   494â†’}

   496â†’func (d *evaluationStateData) GetResource(addr addrs.Resource, rng tfdiags.SourceRange) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   497â†’	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   498â†’	// First we'll consult the configuration to see if an resource of this
   499â†’	// name is declared at all.
   500â†’	moduleAddr := d.ModulePath
   501â†’	moduleConfig := d.Evaluator.Config.DescendantForInstance(moduleAddr)
   502â†’	if moduleConfig == nil {
   503â†’		// should never happen, since we can't be evaluating in a module
   504â†’		// that wasn't mentioned in configuration.
   505â†’		panic(fmt.Sprintf("resource value read from %s, which has no configuration", moduleAddr))
   506â†’	}
   507â†’
   508â†’	config := moduleConfig.Module.ResourceByAddr(addr)
   509â†’	if config == nil {
   510â†’		diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   511â†’			Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   512â†’			Summary:  `Reference to undeclared resource`,
   513â†’			Detail:   fmt.Sprintf(`A resource %q %q has not been declared in %s`, addr.Type, addr.Name, moduleDisplayAddr(moduleAddr)),
   514â†’			Subject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),
   515â†’		})
   516â†’		return cty.DynamicVal, diags
   517â†’	}
   518â†’
   519â†’	// Much of this function was written before we had factored out the handling
   520â†’	// of instance keys into the separate instance expander model, and so it
   521â†’	// does a bunch of instance-related work itself below.
   522â†’	//
   523â†’	// Currently, unknown instance keys are only possible when planning with
   524â†’	// DeferralAllowed set to true in the PlanOpts, which should only be the
   525â†’	// case in the stacks runtime (not the "normal terraform" modules runtime).
   526â†’	// Thus, we have some amount of duplicated code remaining, to be more
   527â†’	// certain that stacks-specific behaviors won't leak out into the standard
   528â†’	// runtime.
   529â†’	//
   530â†’	// TODO: When deferred actions are more stable and robust in stacks, it
   531â†’	// would be nice to rework this function to rely on the ResourceInstanceKeys
   532â†’	// result for _all_ of its work, rather than continuing to duplicate a bunch
   533â†’	// of the logic we've tried to encapsulate over ther already.
   534â†’	if d.Operation == walkPlan || d.Operation == walkApply {
   535â†’		if _, _, hasUnknownKeys := d.Evaluator.Instances.ResourceInstanceKeys(addr.Absolute(moduleAddr)); !hasUnknownKeys {
   536â†’			// There really isn't anything interesting we can do in this situation,
   537â†’			// because it means we have an unknown for_each/count, in which case
   538â†’			// we can't even predict what the result type will be because it
   539â†’			// would be either an object or tuple type decided based on the instance
   540â†’			// keys.
   541â†’			// (We can't get in here for a single-instance resource because in that
   542â†’			// case we would know that there's only one key and it's addrs.NoKey,
   543â†’			// so we'll fall through to the other logic below.)
   544â†’			unknownVal := cty.DynamicVal
   545â†’
   546â†’			// If an ephemeral resource is deferred we need to mark the returned unknown value as ephemeral
   547â†’			if addr.Mode == addrs.EphemeralResourceMode {
   548â†’				unknownVal = unknownVal.Mark(marks.Ephemeral)
   549â†’			}
   550â†’			return unknownVal, diags
   551â†’		}
   552â†’	}
   553â†’
   554â†’	// Build the provider address from configuration, since we may not have
   555â†’	// state available in all cases.
   556â†’	// We need to build an abs provider address, but we can use a default
   557â†’	// instance since we're only interested in the schema.
   558â†’	schema := d.getResourceSchema(addr, config.Provider)
   559â†’	if schema == nil {
   560â†’		// This shouldn't happen, since validation before we get here should've
   561â†’		// taken care of it, but we'll show a reasonable error message anyway.
   562â†’		diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   563â†’			Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   564â†’			Summary:  `Missing resource type schema`,
   565â†’			Detail:   fmt.Sprintf("No schema is available for %s in %s. This is a bug in Terraform and should be reported.", addr, config.Provider),
   566â†’			Subject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),
   567â†’		})
   568â†’		return cty.DynamicVal, diags
   569â†’	}
   570â†’	ty := schema.ImpliedType()
   571â†’
   572â†’	if addr.Mode == addrs.EphemeralResourceMode {
   573â†’		// FIXME: This does not yet work with deferrals, and it would be nice to
   574â†’		// find some way to refactor this so that the following code is not so
   575â†’		// tethered to the current implementation details. Instead we should
   576â†’		// have an abstract idea of first determining what instances the
   577â†’		// resource has (using d.Evaluator.Instances.ResourceInstanceKeys) and
   578â†’		// then retrieving the value for each instance to assemble into the
   579â†’		// result, using some per-resource-mode logic maintained elsewhere.
   580â†’		return d.getEphemeralResource(addr, rng)
   581â†’	}
   582â†’
   583â†’	rs := d.Evaluator.State.Resource(addr.Absolute(d.ModulePath))
   584â†’
   585â†’	if rs == nil {
   586â†’		switch d.Operation {
   587â†’		case walkPlan, walkApply:
   588â†’			// During plan and apply as we evaluate each removed instance they
   589â†’			// are removed from the working state. Since we know there are no
   590â†’			// instances, return an empty container of the expected type.
   591â†’			switch {
   592â†’			case config.Count != nil:
   593â†’				return cty.EmptyTupleVal, diags
   594â†’			case config.ForEach != nil:
   595â†’				return cty.EmptyObjectVal, diags
   596â†’			default:
   597â†’				// While we can reference an expanded resource with 0
   598â†’				// instances, we cannot reference instances that do not exist.
   599â†’				// Due to the fact that we may have direct references to
   600â†’				// instances that may end up in a root output during destroy
   601â†’				// (since a planned destroy cannot yet remove root outputs), we
   602â†’				// need to return a dynamic value here to allow evaluation to
   603â†’				// continue.
   604â†’				log.Printf("[ERROR] unknown instance %q referenced during %s", addr.Absolute(d.ModulePath), d.Operation)
   605â†’				return cty.DynamicVal, diags
   606â†’			}
   607â†’
   608â†’		case walkImport:
   609â†’			// Import does not yet plan resource changes, so new resources from
   610â†’			// config are not going to be found here. Once walkImport fully
   611â†’			// plans resources, this case should not longer be needed.
   612â†’			// In the single instance case, we can return a typed unknown value
   613â†’			// for the instance to better satisfy other expressions using the
   614â†’			// value. This of course will not help if statically known
   615â†’			// attributes are expected to be known elsewhere, but reduces the
   616â†’			// number of problematic configs for now.
   617â†’			// Unlike in plan and apply above we can't be sure the count or
   618â†’			// for_each instances are empty, so we return a DynamicVal. We
   619â†’			// don't really have a good value to return otherwise -- empty
   620â†’			// values will fail for direct index expressions, and unknown
   621â†’			// Lists and Maps could fail in some type unifications.
   622â†’			switch {
   623â†’			case config.Count != nil:
   624â†’				return cty.DynamicVal, diags
   625â†’			case config.ForEach != nil:
   626â†’				return cty.DynamicVal, diags
   627â†’			default:
   628â†’				return cty.UnknownVal(ty), diags
   629â†’			}
   630â†’
   631â†’		default:
   632â†’			// We should only end up here during the validate walk (or
   633â†’			// console/eval), since later walks should have at least partial
   634â†’			// states populated for all resources in the configuration.
   635â†’			return cty.DynamicVal, diags
   636â†’		}
   637â†’	}
   638â†’
   639â†’	// Now, we're going to build up a value that represents the resource
   640â†’	// or resources that are in the state.
   641â†’	instances := map[addrs.InstanceKey]cty.Value{}
   642â†’
   643â†’	// First, we're going to load any instances that we have written into the
   644â†’	// deferrals system. A deferred resource overrides anything that might be
   645â†’	// in the state for the resource, so we do this first.
   646â†’	for key, value := range d.Evaluator.Deferrals.GetDeferredResourceInstances(addr.Absolute(d.ModulePath)) {
   647â†’		instances[key] = value
   648â†’	}
   649â†’
   650â†’	// Decode all instances in the current state
   651â†’	pendingDestroy := d.Operation == walkDestroy
   652â†’	for key, is := range rs.Instances {
   653â†’		if _, ok := instances[key]; ok {
   654â†’			// Then we've already loaded this instance from the deferrals so
   655â†’			// we'll just ignore it being in state.
   656â†’			continue
   657â†’		}
   658â†’		// Otherwise, we'll load the instance from state.
   659â†’
   660â†’		if is == nil || is.Current == nil {
   661â†’			// Assume we're dealing with an instance that hasn't been created yet.
   662â†’			instances[key] = cty.UnknownVal(ty)
   663â†’			continue
   664â†’		}
   665â†’
   666â†’		instAddr := addr.Instance(key).Absolute(d.ModulePath)
   667â†’		change := d.Evaluator.Changes.GetResourceInstanceChange(instAddr, addrs.NotDeposed)
   668â†’		if change != nil {
   669â†’			// Don't take any resources that are yet to be deleted into account.
   670â†’			// If the referenced resource is CreateBeforeDestroy, then orphaned
   671â†’			// instances will be in the state, as they are not destroyed until
   672â†’			// after their dependants are updated.
   673â†’			if change.Action == plans.Delete {
   674â†’				if !pendingDestroy {
   675â†’					continue
   676â†’				}
   677â†’			}
   678â†’		}
   679â†’
   680â†’		// Planned resources are temporarily stored in state with empty values,
   681â†’		// and need to be replaced by the planned value here.
   682â†’		if is.Current.Status == states.ObjectPlanned {
   683â†’			if change == nil {
   684â†’				// FIXME: This is usually an unfortunate case where we need to
   685â†’				// lookup an individual instance referenced via "self" for
   686â†’				// postconditions which we know exists, but because evaluation
   687â†’				// must always get the resource in aggregate some instance
   688â†’				// changes may not yet be registered.
   689â†’				instances[key] = cty.DynamicVal
   690â†’				// log the problem for debugging, since it may be a legitimate error we can't catch
   691â†’				log.Printf("[WARN] instance %s is marked as having a change pending but that change is not recorded in the plan", instAddr)
   692â†’				continue
   693â†’			}
   694â†’			instances[key] = change.After
   695â†’			continue
   696â†’		}
   697â†’
   698â†’		ios, err := is.Current.Decode(ty)
   699â†’		if err != nil {
   700â†’			// This shouldn't happen, since by the time we get here we
   701â†’			// should have upgraded the state data already.
   702â†’			diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   703â†’				Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   704â†’				Summary:  "Invalid resource instance data in state",
   705â†’				Detail:   fmt.Sprintf("Instance %s data could not be decoded from the state: %s.", instAddr, err),
   706â†’				Subject:  &config.DeclRange,
   707â†’			})
   708â†’			continue
   709â†’		}
   710â†’
   711â†’		val := ios.Value
   712â†’
   713â†’		instances[key] = val
   714â†’	}
   715â†’
   716â†’	// ret should be populated with a valid value in all cases below
   717â†’	var ret cty.Value
   718â†’
   719â†’	switch {
   720â†’	case config.Count != nil:
   721â†’		// figure out what the last index we have is
   722â†’		length := -1
   723â†’		for key := range instances {
   724â†’			intKey, ok := key.(addrs.IntKey)
   725â†’			if !ok {
   726â†’				continue
   727â†’			}
   728â†’			if int(intKey) >= length {
   729â†’				length = int(intKey) + 1
   730â†’			}
   731â†’		}
   732â†’
   733â†’		if length > 0 {
   734â†’			vals := make([]cty.Value, length)
   735â†’			for key, instance := range instances {
   736â†’				intKey, ok := key.(addrs.IntKey)
   737â†’				if !ok {
   738â†’					// old key from state, which isn't valid for evaluation
   739â†’					continue
   740â†’				}
   741â†’
   742â†’				vals[int(intKey)] = instance
   743â†’			}
   744â†’
   745â†’			// Insert unknown values where there are any missing instances
   746â†’			for i, v := range vals {
   747â†’				if v == cty.NilVal {
   748â†’					vals[i] = cty.UnknownVal(ty)
   749â†’				}
   750â†’			}
   751â†’			ret = cty.TupleVal(vals)
   752â†’		} else {
   753â†’			ret = cty.EmptyTupleVal
   754â†’		}
   755â†’
   756â†’	case config.ForEach != nil:
   757â†’		vals := make(map[string]cty.Value)
   758â†’		for key, instance := range instances {
   759â†’			strKey, ok := key.(addrs.StringKey)
   760â†’			if !ok {
   761â†’				// old key that is being dropped and not used for evaluation
   762â†’				continue
   763â†’			}
   764â†’			vals[string(strKey)] = instance
   765â†’		}
   766â†’
   767â†’		if len(vals) > 0 {
   768â†’			// We use an object rather than a map here because resource schemas
   769â†’			// may include dynamically-typed attributes, which will then cause
   770â†’			// each instance to potentially have a different runtime type even
   771â†’			// though they all conform to the static schema.
   772â†’			ret = cty.ObjectVal(vals)
   773â†’		} else {
   774â†’			ret = cty.EmptyObjectVal
   775â†’		}
   776â†’
   777â†’	default:
   778â†’		val, ok := instances[addrs.NoKey]
   779â†’		if !ok {
   780â†’			// if the instance is missing, insert an unknown value
   781â†’			val = cty.UnknownVal(ty)
   782â†’		}
   783â†’
   784â†’		ret = val
   785â†’	}
   786â†’
   787â†’	return ret, diags
   788â†’}

   790â†’func (d *evaluationStateData) getEphemeralResource(addr addrs.Resource, rng tfdiags.SourceRange) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   791â†’	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   792â†’
   793â†’	if d.Operation == walkValidate || d.Operation == walkEval {
   794â†’		// Ephemeral instances are never live during the validate walk. Eval is
   795â†’		// similarly offline, and since there is no value stored we can't return
   796â†’		// anything other than dynamic.
   797â†’		return cty.DynamicVal.Mark(marks.Ephemeral), diags
   798â†’	}
   799â†’
   800â†’	// Now, we're going to build up a value that represents the resource
   801â†’	// or resources that are in the state.
   802â†’	instances := map[addrs.InstanceKey]cty.Value{}
   803â†’
   804â†’	// First, we're going to load any instances that we have written into the
   805â†’	// deferrals system. A deferred resource overrides anything that might be
   806â†’	// in the state for the resource, so we do this first.
   807â†’	for key, value := range d.Evaluator.Deferrals.GetDeferredResourceInstances(addr.Absolute(d.ModulePath)) {
   808â†’		instances[key] = value
   809â†’	}
   810â†’
   811â†’	absAddr := addr.Absolute(d.ModulePath)
   812â†’	keyType, keys, haveUnknownKeys := d.Evaluator.Instances.ResourceInstanceKeys(absAddr)
   813â†’	if haveUnknownKeys {
   814â†’		// We can probably do better than totally unknown at least for a
   815â†’		// single-instance resource, but we'll just keep it simple for now.
   816â†’		// Result must be marked as ephemeral so that we can still catch
   817â†’		// attempts to use the results in non-ephemeral locations, so that
   818â†’		// the operator doesn't end up trapped with an error on a subsequent
   819â†’		// plan/apply round.
   820â†’		return cty.DynamicVal.Mark(marks.Ephemeral), diags
   821â†’	}
   822â†’
   823â†’	ephems := d.Evaluator.EphemeralResources
   824â†’	getInstValue := func(addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   825â†’		var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   826â†’
   827â†’		// If we have a deferred instance with this key we don't need to check if it is live or not,
   828â†’		// it has not been created so we can just return the deferred value.
   829â†’		if v, ok := instances[addr.Resource.Key]; ok {
   830â†’			return v, diags
   831â†’		}
   832â†’
   833â†’		val, isLive := ephems.InstanceValue(addr)
   834â†’		if !isLive {
   835â†’			// If the instance is no longer "live" by the time we're accessing
   836â†’			// it then that suggests that it needed renewal and renewal has
   837â†’			// failed, and so the object's value is no longer usable. We'll
   838â†’			// still return the value in case it's somehow useful for diagnosis,
   839â†’			// but we return an error to prevent further evaluation of whatever
   840â†’			// other expression depended on the liveness of this object.
   841â†’			//
   842â†’			// This error message is written on the assumption that it will
   843â†’			// always appear alongside the provider's renewal error, but that'll
   844â†’			// be exposed only once the (now-zombied) ephemeral resource is
   845â†’			// eventually closed, so that we can avoid returning the same error
   846â†’			// multiple times.
   847â†’			diags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{
   848â†’				Severity: hcl.DiagError,
   849â†’				Summary:  "Ephemeral resource instance has expired",
   850â†’				Detail: fmt.Sprintf(
   851â†’					"The remote object for %s is no longer available due to a renewal error, so Terraform cannot evaluate this expression.",
   852â†’					addr,
   853â†’				),
   854â†’				Subject: rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),
   855â†’			})
   856â†’		}
   857â†’		if val == cty.NilVal {
   858â†’			val = cty.DynamicVal.Mark(marks.Ephemeral)
   859â†’		}
   860â†’		return val, diags
   861â†’	}
   862â†’
   863â†’	switch keyType {
   864â†’	case addrs.NoKeyType:
   865â†’		// For "no key" we're returning just a single object representing
   866â†’		// the single instance of this resource.
   867â†’		instVal, moreDiags := getInstValue(absAddr.Instance(addrs.NoKey))
   868â†’		diags = diags.Append(moreDiags)
   869â†’		return instVal, diags
   870â†’	case addrs.IntKeyType:
   871â†’		// For integer keys we're returning a tuple-typed value whose
   872â†’		// indices are the keys.
   873â†’		elems := make([]cty.Value, len(keys))
   874â†’		for _, key := range keys {
   875â†’			idx := int(key.(addrs.IntKey))
   876â†’			instAddr := absAddr.Instance(key)
   877â†’			instVal, moreDiags := getInstValue(instAddr)
   878â†’			diags = diags.Append(moreDiags)
   879â†’			elems[idx] = instVal
   880â†’		}
   881â†’		return cty.TupleVal(elems), diags
   882â†’	case addrs.StringKeyType:
   883â†’		// For string keys we're returning an object-typed value whose
   884â†’		// attributes are the keys.
   885â†’		attrs := make(map[string]cty.Value, len(keys))
   886â†’		for _, key := range keys {
   887â†’			attrName := string(key.(addrs.StringKey))
   888â†’			instAddr := absAddr.Instance(key)
   889â†’			instVal, moreDiags := getInstValue(instAddr)
   890â†’			diags = diags.Append(moreDiags)
   891â†’			attrs[attrName] = instVal
   892â†’		}
   893â†’		return cty.ObjectVal(attrs), diags
   894â†’	default:
   895â†’		panic(fmt.Sprintf("unhandled instance key type %#v", keyType))
   896â†’	}
   897â†’}
   898â†’
   899â†’func (d *evaluationStateData) getResourceSchema(addr addrs.Resource, providerAddr addrs.Provider) *configschema.Block {
   900â†’	schema, _, err := d.Evaluator.Plugins.ResourceTypeSchema(providerAddr, addr.Mode, addr.Type)
   901â†’	if err != nil {
   902â†’		// We have plently other codepaths that will detect and report
   903â†’		// schema lookup errors before we'd reach this point, so we'll just
   904â†’		// treat a failure here the same as having no schema.
   905â†’		return nil
   906â†’	}
   907â†’	return schema
   908â†’}

   910â†’func (d *evaluationStateData) GetOutput(addr addrs.OutputValue, rng tfdiags.SourceRange) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
   911â†’	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
   912â†’
   913â†’	// First we'll make sure the requested value is declared in configuration,
   914â†’	// so we can produce a nice message if not.
   915â†’	moduleConfig := d.Evaluator.Config.DescendantForInstance(d.ModulePath)
   916â†’	if moduleConfig == nil {
   917â†’		// should never happen, since we can't be evaluating in a module
   918â†’		// that wasn't mentioned in configuration.
   919â†’		panic(fmt.Sprintf("output value read from %s, which has no configuration", d.ModulePath))
   920â†’	}
   921â†’
   922â†’	config := moduleConfig.Module.Outputs[addr.Name]
   923â†’	if config == nil {
   924â†’		var suggestions []string
   925â†’		for k := range moduleConfig.Module.Outputs {
   926â†’			suggestions = append(suggestions, k)
   927â†’		}

   965â†’// moduleDisplayAddr returns a string describing the given module instance
